title: Finite Difference Derivative Checks

solver_reuse.target: finite difference derivative bad value QF_LRA/Farkas regression seed
solver_reuse.pressure: Exact rational central-difference replay for f(x)=1+2x+x^2 at x=1 with h=1/2 computes finite_difference_value = 4, while a malformed row claims finite_difference_value = 5, isolating a compact scalar equality conflict for the Farkas route.
solver_reuse.next_step: Promote additional finite-difference rows only when they add distinct stencil-order, boundary-condition, PDE-discretization, floating-point, or theorem-reconstruction pressure; keep truncation-error, convergence, and stability claims in future Lean or numerical-honesty lanes.

graduation_criteria: Encode the fixed polynomial, point, step, stencil offsets, stencil weights, sample values, weighted terms, scale, finite-difference value, and exact derivative value as deterministic rational replay obligations.
Route the false finite-difference value through checked QF_LRA/Farkas evidence after replay computes the exact value.
Expose the pack through derivative, polynomial, calculus, real-analysis, numerical-analysis, and learner query surfaces.

Keep truncation error, convergence order, stability, boundary-condition handling, PDE-discretization correctness, automatic-differentiation behavior, floating-point derivative accuracy, and general finite-difference theory marked as future Lean or numerical-honesty work until formal routes exist.
